content.full_text:
N DIEGO GAS & ELECTRIC COMPANY 
Mr. John Giansello 
SAN 
1714) 
January 20, 1977 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
1100 "K" Street 
Sacramento, California 
95814 
Dear Mr. Giansello: 
On December 4, 1976, it was brought to your 
attention that state government agencies avail themselves 
of my Company's auditorium facilities from time to time. 
These facilities are made available to state agencies on 
the same basis as they are offered to the general public. 
No charge is levied, but use is conditioned upon agree-
ment that no admission be charged by the user and that 
nothing be sold. 
In a general sense, permitted gatherings 
must be of a public service, educational, civic or profes-
sional nature. 
It is our position that such a benefit is neither a 
"gift" nor a "payment to influence legislative or adminis-
trative action" for purposes of the Political Reform Act 
of 1974. 
This conclusion rests on the fact that the transfer 
is made to the agency itself and not to or for the benefit 
of any state official or officer. 
As I understand our conver-
sation of December 4, both you and Rich Eichman concur 
with this result. 
Please note that this advice will be relied upon, 
and no report will be made to the state of any benefit in 
cases such as those described above. 
Should your position 
on this matter change, please advise me. 
Thank you. 
flam 
AN INVt S TOH-UVvNrD CUf1PUlfA nON 
yours, 
.. ,-... ~ 
~\

sections.question_synthetic: Whether providing free use of San Diego Gas & Electric Company's auditorium facilities to state government agencies constitutes a gift or payment to influence legislative or administrative action under the Political Reform Act of 1974.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request/inquiry from a private company to FPPC, not an FPPC response letter. The document describes a conversation with FPPC staff (Mr. Giansello and Rich Eichman) and requests written confirmation of their alleged verbal agreement. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections are present; the question and conclusion are embedded in the body text. The company claims reliance on this advice and states no report will be made unless FPPC changes its position.

embedding.summary: San Diego Gas & Electric Company writes to the FPPC seeking confirmation that providing free auditorium facilities to state agencies does not constitute a gift or improper payment under the Political Reform Act, asserting the benefit transfers to the agency itself rather than individual officials.
